channel.title: volantlabs.ai Perspectives
channel.description: Essays, graph dispatches, and ratified notes behind Vellis.

item.title: The graph is a theory
item.description: Why legibility is the wedge for agentic software, and why the graph should be treated as an executable point of view rather than passive storage.

item.title: Runtime-native controls: policy where execution happens
item.description: A graph-sourced dispatch on why governance must sit near the write path, not in a disconnected approval ritual after the fact.

item.title: Open data, proprietary intelligence
item.description: The line that lets Vellis stay open while proprietary operational intelligence compounds inside each organization.

item.title: Legibility is the wedge for agentic software
item.description: Agents become more useful when the system can show them what things mean, how they relate, and which changes require judgment.

item.title: Why domain explorations matter
item.description: A graph-sourced note on worked models as the bridge between abstract infrastructure and practical adoption.

item.title: No rug-pull means more than a license
item.description: A ratified note on trust signals: portability, export path, and a commercial platform that does not compete with Vellis.
